  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilter"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "FilterHeadersStatus"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "FilterDataStatus"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "FilterTrailersStatus"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h", "symbol": "FactoryBase"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h", "symbol": "CommonFactoryBase"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.h", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilter"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/config.h", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilterConfig"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/cors/cors_filter.h", "symbol": "CorsFilter"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilter::decodeHeaders"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilter::decodeData"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilter::decodeTrailers"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilter::setDecoderFilterCallbacks"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.cc", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilter::decodeHeaders"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.cc", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilter::decodeData"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.cc", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilter::decodeTrailers"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/config.cc", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilterConfig::createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/cors/config.cc", "symbol": "CorsFilterFactory::createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped"}
  ],
  "text": "Implemented a minimal Envoy HTTP pass-through decoder filter following established extension patterns from the envoyproxy/envoy repository (v1.31.2).\n\nKey source files referenced:\n\n1. `envoy/http/filter.h` — Defines the core `Http::StreamDecoderFilter` interface, which requires implementing `decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMap&, bool)`, `decodeData(Buffer::Instance&, bool)`, `decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMap&)`, and `setDecoderFilterCallbacks(StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks&)`. Also defines `FilterHeadersStatus`, `FilterDataStatus`, and `FilterTrailersStatus` return enums.\n\n2. `source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h` — Defines the `FactoryBase<ConfigProto>` template that provides boilerplate for `NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory`. The `PassThroughFilterFactory` inherits from this, using `google::protobuf::Empty` as the config proto since no configuration is needed.\n\n3. `source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.h` and `health_check.cc` — Canonical example of `StreamDecoderFilter` (via `StreamFilter`) with `decodeHeaders`, `decodeData`, and `decodeTrailers` implementations, plus the decoder callbacks pointer pattern.\n\n4. `source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/config.h` and `config.cc` — Shows the `FactoryBase` inheritance pattern, `createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped`, `addStreamFilter`/`addStreamDecoderFilter` callback usage, and the `REGISTER_FACTORY` / `LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY` macro for static registration.\n\n5. `source/extensions/filters/http/cors/cors_filter.h` and `config.cc` — Additional reference showing the same patterns with a simpler filter.\n\nFiles produced:\n- `pass_through_filter.h`: Declares `PassThroughFilter : public Http::StreamDecoderFilter` with `decodeHeaders`, `decodeData`, `decodeTrailers`, `setDecoderFilterCallbacks`, and `onDestroy`.\n- `pass_through_filter.cc`: Implements all three decode methods returning `Continue` status, making this a true pass-through filter.\n- `config.cc`: Defines `PassThroughFilterFactory` inheriting from `Common::FactoryBase<ProtobufWkt::Empty>` with `createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped` that uses `callbacks.addStreamDecoderFilter(...)`, and registers via `REGISTER_FACTORY(PassThroughFilterFactory, Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory)`."
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

# Agentic Correctness: Implement Envoy HTTP Filter Following Extension Patterns

## Your Task

Write a C++ header file `pass_through_filter.h` and implementation file `pass_through_filter.cc` that implement a minimal Envoy HTTP filter following the established extension patterns. Your implementation must: 1. Inherit from `Http::StreamDecoderFilter` as defined in the Envoy source. 2. Implement `decodeHeaders`, `decodeData`, and `decodeTrailers` methods following the same signature conventions as existing filters in `source/extensions/filters/http/`. 3. Register the filter using the `REGISTER_FACTORY` macro pattern. 4. Include a `config.cc` factory file following the `NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory` pattern. Write your files to `/workspace/`. Also write `/workspace/answer.json` listing the Envoy source files you referenced for the filter patterns.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the org domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
- **Keyword presence**: Are required terms present in your explanation?
Now let me look at the StreamDecoderFilter interface and the factory_base:
Now let me look at the `StreamDecoderFilter` interface:
Now I have enough context to implement the files. Let me look at one more example of a simple decoder-only filter:
Now I have enough information to write the implementation. Let me create the files:
Now let me write the `answer.json` with references to all the source files I consulted:

Here's a summary of what I implemented:

## Files Created

### `/workspace/pass_through_filter.h`
Declares `PassThroughFilter` inheriting from `Http::StreamDecoderFilter` with the required interface:
- `decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap&, bool)` → `FilterHeadersStatus`
- `decodeData(Buffer::Instance&, bool)` → `FilterDataStatus`  
- `decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap&)` → `FilterTrailersStatus`
- `setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks&)`
- `onDestroy()` (from `StreamFilterBase`)

### `/workspace/pass_through_filter.cc`
Implements all decode methods returning `Continue`, making it a true no-op pass-through.

### `/workspace/config.cc`
Factory file following the `NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory` pattern:
- `PassThroughFilterFactory` inherits from `Common::FactoryBase<ProtobufWkt::Empty>`
- `createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped` returns a lambda that calls `callbacks.addStreamDecoderFilter(...)`
- Registered via `REGISTER_FACTORY(PassThroughFilterFactory, Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory)`

## Key Source Files Referenced

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `envoy/http/filter.h` | `StreamDecoderFilter` interface definition |
| `source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h` | `FactoryBase<>` template |
| `source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.h/.cc` | Decoder filter method signatures and implementation pattern |
| `source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/config.h/.cc` | Factory config class and `REGISTER_FACTORY` usage |
| `source/extensions/filters/http/cors/cors_filter.h` | Additional decoder filter reference |
| `source/extensions/filters/http/cors/config.cc` | Additional factory registration reference |

{"content":"900: 
901: /**
902:  * Stream decoder filter interface.
903:  */
904: class StreamDecoderFilter : public StreamFilterBase {
905: public:
906:   /**
907:    * Called with decoded headers, optionally indicating end of stream.
908:    * @param headers supplies the decoded headers map.
909:    * @param end_stream supplies whether this is a header only request/response.
910:    * @return FilterHeadersStatus determines how filter chain iteration proceeds.
911:    */
912:   virtual FilterHeadersStatus decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) PURE;
913: 
914:   /**
915:    * Called with a decoded data frame.
916:    * @param data supplies the decoded data.
917:    * @param end_stream supplies whether this is the last data frame.
918:    * Further note that end_stream is only true if there are no trailers.
919:    * @return FilterDataStatus determines how filter chain iteration proceeds.
920:    */
921:   virtual FilterDataStatus decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;
922: 
923:   /**
924:    * Called with decoded trailers, implicitly ending the stream.
925:    * @param trailers supplies the decoded trailers.
926:    */
927:   virtual FilterTrailersStatus decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMap& trailers) PURE;
928: 
929:   /**
930:    * Called with decoded metadata. Add new metadata to metadata_map directly. Do not call
931:    * StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks::addDecodedMetadata() to add new metadata.
932:    *
933:    * Note: decodeMetadata() currently cannot stop the filter iteration, and always returns Continue.
934:    * That means metadata will go through the complete filter chain at once, even if the other frame
935:    * types return StopIteration. If metadata should not pass through all filters at once, users
936:    * should consider using StopAllIterationAndBuffer or StopAllIterationAndWatermark in
937:    * decodeHeaders() to prevent metadata passing to the following filters.
938:    *
939:    * @param metadata_map supplies the decoded metadata.
940:    */
941:   virtual FilterMetadataStatus decodeMetadata(MetadataMap& /* metadata_map */) {
942:     return Http::FilterMetadataStatus::Continue;
943:   }
944: 
945:   /**
946:    * Called by the filter manager once to initialize the filter decoder callbacks that the
947:    * filter should use. Callbacks will not be invoked by the filter after onDestroy() is called.
948:    */
949:   virtual void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;
950: 
951:   /**
952:    * Called at the end of the stream, when all data has been decoded.
953:    */
954:   virtual void decodeComplete() {}
955: };
956: 
957: using StreamDecoderFilterSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<StreamDecoderFilter>;
958: 
959: /**
960:  * Stream encoder filter callbacks add additional callbacks that allow a encoding filter to restart
961: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2@9fad78c5ca13df58ab7b58c0918b8986c9f01efb/-/blob/envoy/http/filter.h%3FL900-960"}

{"content":"1: #pragma once
2: 
3: #include <functional>
4: #include <string>
5: 
6: #include "envoy/config/typed_config.h"
7: #include "envoy/extensions/filters/common/dependency/v3/dependency.pb.h"
8: #include "envoy/http/filter.h"
9: #include "envoy/init/manager.h"
10: #include "envoy/network/filter.h"
11: #include "envoy/server/drain_manager.h"
12: #include "envoy/server/factory_context.h"
13: 
14: #include "source/common/common/assert.h"
15: #include "source/common/common/macros.h"
16: #include "source/common/protobuf/protobuf.h"
17: 
18: namespace Envoy {
19: namespace Server {
20: namespace Configuration {
21: 
22: /**
23:  * Common interface for listener filters and UDP listener filters
24:  */
25: class ListenerFilterConfigFactoryBase : public Config::TypedFactory {
26: public:
27:   ~ListenerFilterConfigFactoryBase() override = default;
28: };
29: 
30: /**
31:  * Implemented by each listener filter and registered via Registry::registerFactory()
32:  * or the convenience class RegisterFactory.
33:  */
34: class NamedListenerFilterConfigFactory : public ListenerFilterConfigFactoryBase {
35: public:
36:   ~NamedListenerFilterConfigFactory() override = default;
37: 
38:   /**
39:    * Create a particular listener filter factory implementation. If the implementation is unable to
40:    * produce a factory with the provided parameters, it should throw an EnvoyException in the case
41:    * of general error or a Json::Exception if the json configuration is erroneous. The returned
42:    * callback should always be initialized.
43:    * @param config supplies the general protobuf configuration for the filter.
44:    * @param listener_filter_matcher supplies the matcher to decide when filter is enabled.
45:    * @param context supplies the filter's context.
46:    * @return Network::ListenerFilterFactoryCb the factory creation function.
47:    */
48:   virtual Network::ListenerFilterFactoryCb createListenerFilterFactoryFromProto(
49:       const Protobuf::Message& config,
50:       const Network::ListenerFilterMatcherSharedPtr& listener_filter_matcher,
51: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2@9fad78c5ca13df58ab7b58c0918b8986c9f01efb/-/blob/envoy/server/filter_config.h%3FL1-50"}

    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/cors/cors_filter.h", "symbol": "CorsFilter"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilter::decodeHeaders"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilter::decodeData"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilter::decodeTrailers"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "envoy/http/filter.h", "symbol": "StreamDecoderFilter::setDecoderFilterCallbacks"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.cc", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilter::decodeHeaders"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.cc", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilter::decodeData"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.cc", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilter::decodeTrailers"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/config.cc", "symbol": "HealthCheckFilterConfig::createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped"},
    {"repo": "envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/cors/config.cc", "symbol": "CorsFilterFactory::createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped"}
  ],
  "text": "Implemented a minimal Envoy HTTP pass-through decoder filter following established extension patterns from the envoyproxy/envoy repository (v1.31.2).\n\nKey source files referenced:\n\n1. `envoy/http/filter.h` — Defines the core `Http::StreamDecoderFilter` interface, which requires implementing `decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMap&, bool)`, `decodeData(Buffer::Instance&, bool)`, `decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMap&)`, and `setDecoderFilterCallbacks(StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks&)`. Also defines `FilterHeadersStatus`, `FilterDataStatus`, and `FilterTrailersStatus` return enums.\n\n2. `source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h` — Defines the `FactoryBase<ConfigProto>` template that provides boilerplate for `NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory`. The `PassThroughFilterFactory` inherits from this, using `google::protobuf::Empty` as the config proto since no configuration is needed.\n\n3. `source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.h` and `health_check.cc` — Canonical example of `StreamDecoderFilter` (via `StreamFilter`) with `decodeHeaders`, `decodeData`, and `decodeTrailers` implementations, plus the decoder callbacks pointer pattern.\n\n4. `source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/config.h` and `config.cc` — Shows the `FactoryBase` inheritance pattern, `createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped`, `addStreamFilter`/`addStreamDecoderFilter` callback usage, and the `REGISTER_FACTORY` / `LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY` macro for static registration.\n\n5. `source/extensions/filters/http/cors/cors_filter.h` and `config.cc` — Additional reference showing the same patterns with a simpler filter.\n\nFiles produced:\n- `pass_through_filter.h`: Declares `PassThroughFilter : public Http::StreamDecoderFilter` with `decodeHeaders`, `decodeData`, `decodeTrailers`, `setDecoderFilterCallbacks`, and `onDestroy`.\n- `pass_through_filter.cc`: Implements all three decode methods returning `Continue` status, making this a true pass-through filter.\n- `config.cc`: Defines `PassThroughFilterFactory` inheriting from `Common::FactoryBase<ProtobufWkt::Empty>` with `createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped` that uses `callbacks.addStreamDecoderFilter(...)`, and registers via `REGISTER_FACTORY(PassThroughFilterFactory, Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory)`."
}

The `PassThroughFilterFactory` inherits from this, using `google::protobuf::Empty` as the config proto since no configuration is needed.\n\n3. `source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/health_check.h` and `health_check.cc` — Canonical example of `StreamDecoderFilter` (via `StreamFilter`) with `decodeHeaders`, `decodeData`, and `decodeTrailers` implementations, plus the decoder callbacks pointer pattern.\n\n4. `source/extensions/filters/http/health_check/config.h` and `config.cc` — Shows the `FactoryBase` inheritance pattern, `createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped`, `addStreamFilter`/`addStreamDecoderFilter` callback usage, and the `REGISTER_FACTORY` / `LEGACY_REGISTER_FACTORY` macro for static registration.\n\n5. `source/extensions/filters/http/cors/cors_filter.h` and `config.cc` — Additional reference showing the same patterns with a simpler filter.\n\nFiles produced:\n- `pass_through_filter.h`: Declares `PassThroughFilter : public Http::StreamDecoderFilter` with `decodeHeaders`, `decodeData`, `decodeTrailers`, `setDecoderFilterCallbacks`, and `onDestroy`.\n- `pass_through_filter.cc`: Implements all three decode methods returning `Continue` status, making this a true pass-through filter.\n- `config.cc`: Defines `PassThroughFilterFactory` inheriting from `Common::FactoryBase<ProtobufWkt::Empty>` with `createFilterFactoryFromProtoTyped` that uses `callbacks.addStreamDecoderFilter(...)`, and registers via `REGISTER_FACTORY(PassThroughFilterFactory, Server::Configuration::NamedHttpFilterConfigFactory)`."
}
